meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve App Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop building apps that lack data. Learn how to implement professional analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance business today.
content.answer_block: To break through the 'basic app' barrier, you must move beyond CRUD operations and implement event-driven analytics. By integrating a lightweight tracking layer using Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform static code into a data-backed product, allowing you to prove ROI to clients and justify higher project fees instantly.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the client loves the UI, but the app feels like a ghost town. You’re shipping features, yet you have zero visibility into how users actually interact with your code. It’s the classic freelance trap—building impressive shells that lack the data-driven heartbeat required to scale or secure long-term retainers.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to bridge the gap between 'just another app' and 'data-driven solution' using a high-velocity stack. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you will turn your next project into a professional-grade analytics engine in under an hour.

steps.title: Define Your Event Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define exactly what user behavior translates to business value. We focus on 'High-Intent' events like sign-ups, feature toggles, and conversion points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS analytics tracking system. Include events for user_signup, button_click, and page_view. Map these to a PostHog-style event structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate an analytics_schema.ts file based on the provided event definitions.'

steps.title: Inject the Tracking Provider
steps.description: We need to wrap our app in a provider that captures events without polluting the business logic. This keeps your code clean and modular.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a React AnalyticsProvider component that uses a context API to expose a trackEvent function. Ensure it handles async event batching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap the main App component in the new AnalyticsProvider and expose the trackEvent hook.'

steps.title: Automate Event Binding
steps.description: Instead of manual entry, we use Claude Code to scan the project and attach tracking to all primary CTA buttons automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom hook useTrackClick that accepts an event name and properties, returning an onClick handler that triggers the tracking event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find all primary buttons in the components folder and replace their onClick handlers with the useTrackClick hook.'

steps.title: Implement Middleware for Routing
steps.description: Tracking page views is essential for funnel analysis. We implement a router listener to fire events whenever the URL changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a useEffect hook for a React Router setup that fires a 'page_view' event every time location.pathname changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the page view tracking hook into the main router configuration.'

steps.title: Final Validation and Export
steps.description: We verify that events are firing correctly and prepare the analytics layer for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock test suite to verify that trackEvent is called with correct payloads when buttons are clicked.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the test suite and confirm all tracking events are logged correctly in the console.'

pro_tips: Always debounce your event tracking to prevent API rate limiting on high-traffic apps.
Tag your events with the user's plan tier to segment analytics data by revenue value.
Use a 'staging' flag in your analytics provider to keep dev data out of your production dashboard.
Capture error events as custom analytics events to proactively debug issues before the client reports them.
Store user session IDs in local storage to track user journeys across multiple visits.
content.next_steps: Now that your tracking is live, deploy your app to your staging environment and click through your user flows. Verify the events in your analytics dashboard, then present the data-driven dashboard to your client as a value-add service.
content.conversion_block: Stop building blind. Start delivering apps that provide actionable insights and command higher rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
